29	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
30	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
31	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
32	const (
33		tryExitSuccess         = 0 // success or already_satisfied
34		tryExitPreservedOrNone = 1 // preserved_for_review or no_changes
35		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
36	)
37	
38	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
39	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
40	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
41	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
42	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
43		cmd := &cobra.Command{
44			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
45			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
46			SilenceUsage: true,
47			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
48	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
49	
50	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
51	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
52	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
53	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
54	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
55	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
56	
57	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
58	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
59	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
60	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
61	
62	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
63	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
64	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
65	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
66	
67	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
68	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
69	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
70	
71	Exit codes:
72	  0  success or already_satisfied
73	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
74	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
75	`,
76			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
77	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
78	
79	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
80	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
81	
82	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
83	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
84			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
85			RunE: f.runTry,
86		}
87	
88		// Routing knobs — identical to "ddx work" passthrough surface.
89		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
90		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
91		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
92		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
93		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
94		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
95		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
96		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-backend", "", "Attempt backend: worktree, local-clone, docker-clone, or in-tree (default: executions.attempt_backend)")
97		cmd.Flags().Bool("force-claim", false, "Ignore retry cooldown for this targeted attempt only (requires --reason)")
98		cmd.Flags().String("reason", "", "Operator reason required by --force-claim")
99		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
100		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
101		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
102		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart powerClass)")
103		cmd.Flags().Duration("preclaim-timeout", workguard.DefaultPreClaimTimeout, "Pre-claim readiness timeout for preflight/readiness hooks")
104		cmd.Flags().Duration("route-resolution-timeout", agent.DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout, "Timeout bounding routing preflight and the resolveRoute viability check; on expiry the lease is released and the bead is flagged for operator attention")
105		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
106		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
107		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
108	
109		return cmd
110	}

112	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
113	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
114		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
115			return err
116		}
117	
118		beadID := args[0]
119	
120		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
121		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
122		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
123	
124		if _, err := newStartupHousekeepingRunner(projectRoot).Cleanup(cmd.Context()); err != nil {
125			return err
126		}
127	
128		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
129		// Runs before claim/worktree setup so the operator sees the warning before
130		// any bead state changes.
131		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
132		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
133			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
134		})
135	
136		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
137		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
138		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
139		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
140		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
141		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
142		attemptBackend, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-backend")
143		forceClaim, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force-claim")
144		forceReason, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("reason")
145		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
146		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
147		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
148		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
149		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
150		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
151		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
152	
153		forceReason = strings.TrimSpace(forceReason)
154		if noReview && !noReviewAck {
155			return fmt.Errorf("--no-review requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing (break-glass acknowledgement)")
156		}
157		if forceClaim && forceReason == "" {
158			return fmt.Errorf("--force-claim requires --reason \"<text>\"")
159		}
160	
161		if f.tryExecutorOverride == nil {
162			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider); err != nil {
163				return err
164			}
165		}
166	
167		store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
168	
169		// Pre-flight: look up the bead.
170		target, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
171		if err != nil {
172			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead not found: %s\n", beadID)
173			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
174		}
175	
176		// Pre-flight: verify the bead is in a claimable state.
177		// Claimable = open, or stale in_progress (the loop handles stale reclaim).
178		switch target.Status {
179		case bead.StatusOpen, bead.StatusInProgress:
180			// allowed — loop's Claim() handles stale-heartbeat reclaim
181		default:
182			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not claimable (status=%s)\n", target.Status)
183			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
184		}
185	
186		// Pre-flight: check for unmet dependencies.
187		if unmet := unmetDeps(store, target); len(unmet) > 0 {
188			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead has unmet dependencies: %s\n", strings.Join(unmet, ", "))
189			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
190		}
191	
192		// Restrict the ready queue view to the targeted bead. Retry cooldown is
193		// only bypassed when --force-claim is explicitly present.
194		forcedStore := &singleBeadStore{
195			Store:         store,
196			targetID:      target.ID,
197			forceCooldown: forceClaim,
198		}
199		if !forceClaim {
200			standardReady, standardErr := store.ReadyExecution()
201			if standardErr == nil && !containsBeadID(standardReady, target.ID) {
202				if forcedReady, readyErr := store.ReadyExecutionIgnoringCooldown(); readyErr == nil && containsBeadID(forcedReady, target.ID) {
203					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is in retry cooldown; re-run with --force-claim --reason \"<text>\": %s\n", beadID)
204					return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
205				}
206			}
207		}
208	
209		// Set up progress logging (same as runAgentExecuteLoopImpl).
210		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
211		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
212		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
213		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
214		var loopSink io.Writer
215		if lf, openErr := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); openErr == nil {
216			loopSink = lf
217			defer lf.Close() //nolint:errcheck
218		}
219	
220		// Worker-probe (best-effort server mirror; non-blocking).
221		probeIdent := workerprobe.Identity{
222			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
223			Harness:      harness,
224			Model:        model,
225			ExecutorPID:  os.Getpid(),
226			ExecutorHost: hostnameOrEmpty(),
227			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
228		}
229		probe := workerprobe.New(probeIdent, workerprobe.Config{
230			AddrFunc: serverpkg.ReadServerAddr,
231		})
232		probeCtx, probeCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
233		probe.Start(probeCtx)
234		defer func() {
235			probeCancel()
236			probe.Stop()
237		}()
238		if loopSink != nil {
239			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(loopSink, probe)
240		} else {
241			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(io.Discard, probe)
242		}
243	
244		// Process-local LandCoordinator.
245		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
246		defer localCoord.Stop()
247	
248		// Post-merge reviewer (on by default, skipped via --no-review).
249		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
250		if !noReview {
251			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
252				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
253				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot)),
254				BeadEvents:  bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot)),
255				Harness:     reviewHarness,
256				Model:       reviewModel,
257			}
258		}
259	
260		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
261	
262		// Determine whether zero-config auto-route applies (same logic as runWork).
263		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" &&
264			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile") && !cmd.Flags().Changed("min-power") &&
265			!cmd.Flags().Changed("max-power")
266		hasProjectRoutingConfig := projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
267		autoInferPowerClass := noRoutingFlags && !hasProjectRoutingConfig
268		var ladderOnce sync.Once
269		var ladder escalationFloorFinder
270		loadLadder := func() escalationFloorFinder {
271			ladderOnce.Do(func() {
272				ladder = powerladder.NewLadder(nil)
273				svc, svcErr := agent.ResolveServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
274				if svcErr != nil {
275					return
276				}
277				modelCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(cmd.Context(), 2*time.Second)
278				defer cancel()
279				models, listErr := svc.ListModels(modelCtx, agentlib.ModelFilter{})
280				if listErr != nil {
281					return
282				}
283				ladder = powerladder.NewLadder(models)
284			})
285			return ladder
286		}
287		resourceChecker := buildCLIResourceChecker(projectRoot, f.resourceCheckerOverride)
288		profileSelector := newImplementationProfileSelector(projectRoot)
289	
290		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
291		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
292			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
293		}
294		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
295	
296		// Build the executor: either the test override or the real single-power executor.
297		var executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
298		if f.tryExecutorOverride != nil {
299			executor = f.tryExecutorOverride
300		} else {
301			executor = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, execBeadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
302				singleAttempt := func(ctx context.Context, execBeadID string, requestMinPower int) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
303					requestProfile := profile
304					var routingNote string
305					targetBead := target
306					if b, getErr := store.Get(context.Background(), execBeadID); getErr == nil {
307						targetBead = b
308					}
309					routingIntent := resolveCommandExecutionHint(ctx, targetBead, noRoutingFlags, hasProjectRoutingConfig)
310					inferredPolicy := routingIntent.InferredPowerClass
311					reportFromResult := func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
312						report := agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(routingIntent.InferredPowerClass))
313						applyExecutionRoutingIntentReport(&report, routingIntent, requestProfile, routingNote)
314						return report
315					}
316					if autoInferPowerClass {
317						var unavailableReport agent.ExecuteBeadReport
318						var unavailable bool
319						if selection, selectErr := profileSelector.Select(ctx, inferredPolicy, requestMinPower); selectErr == nil && selection.Name != "" {
320							requestProfile = selection.Name
321							routingNote = selection.Note
322							if maxPower > 0 && requestMinPower > 0 && requestMinPower >= maxPower {
323								unavailableReport := routeUnavailableReport(targetBead, requestMinPower, maxPower, nil)
324								applyExecutionRoutingIntentReport(&unavailableReport, routingIntent, requestProfile, routingNote)
325								return unavailableReport, nil
326							}
327						} else {
328							requestMinPower, unavailableReport, unavailable = zeroConfigInitialMinPower(targetBead, inferredPolicy, requestMinPower, maxPower, loadLadder())
329							if unavailable {
330								applyExecutionRoutingIntentReport(&unavailableReport, routingIntent, "", "")
331								return unavailableReport, nil
332							}
333						}
334					}
335	
336					loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
337						Harness:           harness,
338						Model:             model,
339						Provider:          provider,
340						Profile:           requestProfile,
341						Effort:            effort,
342						MinPower:          requestMinPower,
343						MaxPower:          maxPower,
344						OpaquePassthrough: true, // ddx try treats flags as opaque passthrough
345						AttemptBackend:    attemptBackend,
346					}
347					if requestTimeout > 0 {
348						loopOverrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
349					}
350					attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, loopOverrides)
351					res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, execBeadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
352						FromRev:         fromRev,
353						Output:          cmd.OutOrStdout(),
354						BeadEvents:      bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot)),
355						ResourceChecker: resourceChecker,
356					}, gitOps)
357					// Safety net: commit any execution-evidence bundle that
358					// ExecuteBeadWithConfig published to the project root but the land
359					// flow did not pick up — e.g. a terminal provider_connectivity /
360					// route-failure that never produced a commit to land. Without this
361					// the project worktree is left dirty after the tracker commit
362					// already landed (ddx-ca94d157). Mirrors the server worker path
363					// (internal/server/workers.go).
364					if res != nil && res.AttemptID != "" {
365						defer func(attemptID string) {
366							_ = agent.VerifyCleanWorktree(projectRoot, attemptID)
367						}(res.AttemptID)
368					}
369					if execErr != nil && res == nil {
370						return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
371					}
372					if res != nil && agent.IsResourceExhaustedStatus(res.Status) {
373						return reportFromResult(res), nil
374					}
375					if execErr != nil {
376						agent.MarkResultExecutionError(res, execErr)
377						return reportFromResult(res), nil
378					}
379					if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
380						if wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps); ctxErr != nil {
381							fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
382						} else if wt != "" {
383							defer cleanup()
384							checksAbs := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
385							checksRel := filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
386							anyFailed, ratchetFailed, evalErr := agent.EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(wt, ids, res, projectRoot, checksAbs, checksRel)
387							if evalErr != nil {
388								fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "ddx: warning: gate evaluation failed: %v (skipping)\n", evalErr)
389							} else if anyFailed || ratchetFailed {
390								preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
391								if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, preserveRef, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
392									agent.MarkResultLandError(projectRoot, res, upErr)
393								} else {
394									res.PreserveRef = preserveRef
395									res.Outcome = "preserved"
396									if ratchetFailed {
397										res.Reason = agent.RatchetPreserveReason
398									} else {
399										res.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
400									}
401									res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
402									res.Detail = agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
403								}
404								_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
405								report := agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(routingIntent.InferredPowerClass))
406								applyExecutionRoutingIntentReport(&report, routingIntent, requestProfile, routingNote)
407								return report, nil
408							}
409						}
410						targetBead := target
411						landRes, _, landErr := agent.SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
412							ctx, projectRoot, targetBead, res,
413							func(req agent.LandRequest) (*agent.LandResult, error) { return localCoord.Submit(req) },
414							bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot)),
415							resolveClaimAssignee(), "ddx try",
416							nil,
417						)
418						if landErr == nil {
419							agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)
420							_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
421						} else {
422							agent.MarkResultLandError(projectRoot, res, landErr)
423							_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
424							return reportFromResult(res), nil
425						}
426					} else if res != nil && (res.Outcome == agent.ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed || res.ExitCode != 0) {
427						if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
428							res.Outcome = "preserved"
429						} else {
430							res.Outcome = "error"
431						}
432						res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
433					} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
434						res.Outcome = "no-changes"
435						res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
436					}
437					report := agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(routingIntent.InferredPowerClass))
438					applyExecutionRoutingIntentReport(&report, routingIntent, requestProfile, routingNote)
439					return report, nil
440				}
441				return runEscalatingPowerAttempts(ctx, minPower, loadLadder(), func(ctx context.Context, requestedMinPower int) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
442					return singleAttempt(ctx, execBeadID, requestedMinPower)
443				}, nil, nil, strings.TrimSpace(harness) == "" && strings.TrimSpace(provider) == "" && strings.TrimSpace(model) == "")
444			})
445		}
446	
447		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
448			Store:    forcedStore,
449			Executor: executor,
450			Reviewer: reviewer,
451		}
452	
453		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
454			Assignee:       resolveClaimAssignee(),
455			Harness:        harness,
456			Model:          model,
457			Provider:       provider,
458			Profile:        profile,
459			Effort:         effort,
460			AttemptBackend: attemptBackend,
461		}
462		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
463		if err != nil {
464			return fmt.Errorf("load resolved config: %w", err)
465		}
466		if _, err := resourceChecker.Check(cmd.Context()); err != nil {
467			if resErr, ok := err.(*agent.ResourceExhaustedError); ok && resErr != nil {
468				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), resErr.Error())
469			}
470			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: err.Error()}
471		}
472	
473		cliLandingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
474		var qualityRunner agent.AgentRunner
475		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
476			qualityRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
477		}
478		lintHook := agent.NewPreDispatchLintHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner)
479		triageHook := agent.NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner, nil)
480		proseHook := f.proseEvidenceHookOverride
481		if proseHook == nil {
482			proseHook = agent.NewDefaultProseEvidenceHook(agent.ProseEvidenceConfig{
483				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
484				Events:      bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot)),
485				Actor:       resolveClaimAssignee(),
486				Source:      "ddx try",
487			})
488		}
489		result, runErr := worker.Run(cmd.Context(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
490			Mode:                   executeloop.ModeOnce,
491			IgnoreCooldown:         forceClaim,
492			CooldownOverrideReason: forceReason,
493			Log:                    cmd.OutOrStdout(),
494			EventSink:              loopSink,
495			WorkerID:               resolveClaimAssignee(),
496			ProjectRoot:            projectRoot,
497			ResourceChecker:        resourceChecker,
498			SessionID:              loopSessionID,
499			PreClaimHook:           buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps),
500			PreDispatchLintHook:    lintHook,
501			PostAttemptTriageHook:  triageHook,
502			ProseEvidenceHook:      proseHook,
503			FinalizeDurableAudit:   f.buildAttemptAuditFinalizer(projectRoot, store),
504			NoReview:               noReview,
505		})
506		if runErr != nil {
507			if (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(runErr, context.DeadlineExceeded)) && result != nil && len(result.Results) > 0 {
508				writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Results[0])
509			}
510			return runErr
511		}
512	
513		// Report outcome.
514		if result.NoReadyWork {
515			// Bead was not in the execution-ready queue even after pre-flight passed.
516			// This can happen if a heartbeat re-appeared just before claim, making it
517			// not stale-enough for reclaim. Surface a clear message.
518			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
519			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
520		}
521	
522		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
523			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
524		}
525		report := result.Results[0]
526		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
527		if report.EstimatedDifficulty != "" && report.InferredPowerClass != "" {
528			source := report.RoutingIntentSource
529			if source == "" {
530				source = string(escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceDefault)
531			}
532			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: difficulty=%s powerClass=%s source=%s\n", report.EstimatedDifficulty, report.InferredPowerClass, source)
533		}
534	
535		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
536	}

538	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
539		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
540		if report.Detail != "" {
541			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
542		}
543		if report.ResultRev != "" {
544			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
545		}
546		if report.ImplementationRev != "" && report.ImplementationRev != report.LandedRev {
547			fmt.Fprintf(w, "implementation_rev: %s\n", report.ImplementationRev)
548		}
549		if report.LandedRev != "" && report.LandedRev != report.ImplementationRev {
550			fmt.Fprintf(w, "landed_rev: %s\n", report.LandedRev)
551		}
552		if isSuccessfulTryResult(report) && report.TargetBranch != "" {
553			fmt.Fprintf(w, "landed_branch: %s\n", report.TargetBranch)
554		}
555		if isSuccessfulTryResult(report) && report.ProjectRoot != "" {
556			fmt.Fprintf(w, "project_root: %s\n", report.ProjectRoot)
557		}
558		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
559			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
560		}
561		if needsNoRewriteLandingRescue(report) {
562			fmt.Fprintf(w, "landing_scope: branch-local\n")
563			fmt.Fprintf(w, "rescue_command: %s\n", noRewriteLandingRescueCommand(report.TargetBranch))
564			fmt.Fprintf(w, "dirty_worktree_rescue: %s\n", dirtyWorktreeRescueCommand(report.TargetBranch))
565		}
566	}
567	
568	func isSuccessfulTryResult(report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
569		return report.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
570	}
571	
572	func needsNoRewriteLandingRescue(report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
573		if !isSuccessfulTryResult(report) || report.TargetBranch == "" {
574			return false
575		}
576		switch report.TargetBranch {
577		case "main", "master", "trunk":
578			return false
579		default:
580			return true
581		}
582	}
583	
584	func noRewriteLandingRescueCommand(branch string) string {
585		return "git merge --no-ff " + branch
586	}
587	
588	func dirtyWorktreeRescueCommand(branch string) string {
589		safeBranch := strings.NewReplacer("/", "-", "\\", "-", " ", "-").Replace(branch)
590		return fmt.Sprintf("git worktree add ../ddx-rescue-%s <target-branch> && cd ../ddx-rescue-%s && git merge --no-ff %s", safeBranch, safeBranch, branch)
591	}

593	// tryExitCodeForStatus maps a bead execution status string to the ddx try exit code contract.
594	func tryExitCodeForStatus(status string) error {
595		switch status {
596		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
597			return nil // exit 0
598		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
599			agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced:
600			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitPreservedOrNone, Message: ""}
601		default:
602			// execution_failed and all other terminal non-success statuses
603			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
604		}
605	}

607	// unmetDeps returns the IDs of deps that are not yet closed, in the order
608	// they appear in the bead's dependency list.
609	func unmetDeps(store *bead.Store, b *bead.Bead) []string {
610		depIDs := b.DepIDs()
611		if len(depIDs) == 0 {
612			return nil
613		}
614		var unmet []string
615		for _, depID := range depIDs {
616			dep, err := store.Get(context.Background(), depID)
617			if err != nil || dep.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
618				unmet = append(unmet, depID)
619			}
620		}
621		return unmet
622	}

18	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
19	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
20	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
21	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.

23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "work",
25			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
26			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
27	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
28	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
29	
30	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
31	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
32	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
33	bounds.
34	
35	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
36	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
37	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
38	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
39	
40	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
41	  drained     — no ready beads remain
42	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
43	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
44	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
45	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
46	
47	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
48	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
49	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
50	`,
51			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
52	  ddx work

127	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
128	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
129	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
130	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
131	// by this layer.
132	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
133		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
134			return err
135		}
136	
137		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
138		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
139		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
140		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
141		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
142	
143		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
144		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
145		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
146		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
147		if !asJSON {
148			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
149			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
150				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
151			})
152		}
153	
154		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
155	}

169		}
170	
171		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
172			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
173			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
174			return nil
175		}
176	
177		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
178		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
179		writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w, result.OperatorAttention)
180		if result.Failures > 0 {
181			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
182			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
183				if attempt.Status != "success" {
184					fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
185					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
186						fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
187					}
188					fmt.Fprintln(w)
189				}
190			}
191		}
192		if result.NoReadyWork {
193			fmt.Fprintln(w)
194			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
195			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
196		}
197		return nil
198	}
199	
200	func writeWorkTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) {
201		closed, changed, alreadySatisfied := countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result)
202		fmt.Fprintf(w, "worker exited: %s\n", workExitSummary(result))
203		fmt.Fprintf(w, "attempts: %d  |  closed: %d  |  changed: %d  |  already-satisfied: %d  |  failures: %d\n",
204			result.Attempts, closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, result.Failures)
205	}
206	
207	func writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w io.Writer, stop *agent.OperatorAttentionStop) {
208		if stop == nil {
209			return
210		}
211		fmt.Fprintln(w)
212		fmt.Fprintln(w, "operator attention:")
213		if stop.BeadID != "" {
214			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  released bead: %s\n", stop.BeadID)
215		}
216		if stop.Message != "" {
217			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %s\n", stop.Message)
218		}
219		if stop.ProjectRoot != "" {
220			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  project root: %s\n", stop.ProjectRoot)
221		}
222		if len(stop.DirtyPaths) > 0 {
223			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  dirty paths: %s\n", strings.Join(stop.DirtyPaths, ", "))
224		}
225	}
226	
227	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied int) {
228		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
229			switch attempt.Status {
230			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
231				closed++
232				changed++
233			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
234				closed++
235				alreadySatisfied++
236			}
237		}
238		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
239			closed = result.Successes
240		}
241		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied
242	}
243	
244	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
245		switch result.ExitReason {
246		case "drained":
247			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
248		case "once_complete":
249			return "once complete"
250		case "budget":
251			return "budget reached"
252		case "no_progress":
253			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
254		case "blocked":
255			return "blocked waiting for external action"
256		case "operator_attention":
257			return "operator attention required"
258		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
259			return "stopped by signal"
260		case "fatal_config":
261			return "fatal configuration error"
262		case "preflight_failed":
263			return "preflight failed"
264		case "resource_exhausted":
265			return "resource exhausted"
266		case "routing_unavailable":
267			return "routing unavailable"
268		}
269		if result.StopCondition != "" {
270			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
271		}
272		return "completed"
273	}
274	
275	func writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, snapshot *agent.QueueSnapshot) {
276		if snapshot == nil {
277			return
278		}
279		retry := ""
280		if snapshot.NextRetryAfter != "" {
281			retry = " next-retry=" + snapshot.NextRetryAfter
282		}
283		fmt.Fprintf(w, "remaining queue: execution-ready=%d blocked=%d operator-attention=%d needs-human/investigation=%d cooldown/deferred=%d%s execution-ineligible=%d superseded=%d epics=%d epic-closure-candidates=%d\n",
284			snapshot.ExecutionReadyCount,
285			snapshot.BlockedCount,
286			snapshot.ProposedOperatorAttentionCount,
287			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount,
288			snapshot.RetryCooldownCount,
289			retry,
290			snapshot.ExecutionIneligibleCount,
291			snapshot.SupersededCount,
292			snapshot.SkippedEpicsCount,
293			snapshot.EpicClosureCandidatesCount,
294		)
295		if snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal <= 0 || snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount <= 0 {
296			return
297		}
298		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%d beads blocked behind %d needs-human blockers:\n",
299			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal, snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount)
300		for i, blocker := range snapshot.HumanReviewBlockers {
301			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %d. %s %s (%d downstream)\n", i+1, blocker.ID, blocker.Title, blocker.DownstreamBlockedCount)
302		}
303	}
304	
305	func writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) {
306		if projectRoot != "" {
307			fmt.Fprintf(w, "project: %s\n", projectRoot)
308		}
309		fmt.Fprintln(w, "No execution-ready beads.")
310		if noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d) {
311			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  queue drained: no open work remains in lifecycle queues.")
312		}
313		if len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) > 0 {
314			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  operator attention: %d proposed bead(s) stop autonomous work and may block downstream dependents: %s\n",
315				len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention), strings.Join(d.ProposedOperatorAttention, ", "))
316		}
317		if len(d.DependencyWaiting) > 0 {
318			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  waiting on dependencies: %d open bead(s): %s\n",
319				len(d.DependencyWaiting), strings.Join(d.DependencyWaiting, ", "))
320		}
321		if len(d.ExternalBlocked) > 0 {
322			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  external blocked: %d bead(s) with explicit blocked status: %s\n",
323				len(d.ExternalBlocked), strings.Join(d.ExternalBlocked, ", "))
324		}
325		if len(d.RetryCooldown) > 0 {
326			retryHint := ""
327			if d.NextRetryAfter != "" {
328				retryHint = " (next retry-after: " + d.NextRetryAfter + ")"
329			}
330			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  retry cooldown: %d bead(s)%s: %s\n",
331				len(d.RetryCooldown), retryHint, strings.Join(d.RetryCooldown, ", "))
332		}
333		if len(d.NotEligible) > 0 {
334			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  not execution eligible: %d bead(s): %s\n",
335				len(d.NotEligible), strings.Join(d.NotEligible, ", "))
336		}
337		if len(d.Superseded) > 0 {
338			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  superseded: %d bead(s): %s\n",
339				len(d.Superseded), strings.Join(d.Superseded, ", "))
340		}
341		if len(d.Epics) > 0 {
342			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  epic containers: %d ready epic(s) with open children (decompose into tasks): %s\n",
343				len(d.Epics), strings.Join(d.Epics, ", "))
344		}
345		if len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) > 0 {
346			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  completed epic closure candidate(s) (all direct children closed; surfaced for closure evaluation): %s\n",
347				strings.Join(d.EpicClosureCandidates, ", "))
348		}
349	}
350	
351	func noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) bool {
352		return len(d.ExecutionReady) == 0 &&
353			len(d.DependencyWaiting) == 0 &&
354			len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) == 0 &&
355			len(d.RetryCooldown) == 0 &&
356			len(d.ExternalBlocked) == 0 &&
357			len(d.NotEligible) == 0 &&
358			len(d.Superseded) == 0 &&
359			len(d.Epics) == 0 &&
360			len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) == 0 &&
361			d.NextRetryAfter == ""
362	}

18	// newRunCommand creates the top-level "ddx run" command — the layer-1 task
19	// invocation primitive. One Fizeau Execute call; no queue semantics, no
20	// routing policy, no ResolveRoute. Power bounds and passthrough constraints
21	// are forwarded unchanged (CONTRACT-003).
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newRunCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:          "run",
25			Short:        "Invoke a task dispatcher for a prompt (layer-1 Fizeau Execute call)",
26			SilenceUsage: true,
27			Long: `run dispatches a single task to the upstream agent service via one Fizeau
28	Execute call. It is the layer-1 primitive over which ddx try and ddx work add
29	bead lifecycle, queue semantics, and retry policy.
30	
31	DDx passes all routing knobs (--harness, --provider, --model,
32	--min-power, --max-power, --profile) directly to the service without validation
33	or pre-resolution. The service owns route selection within those bounds
34	(CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010).
35	
36	Persona injection: --persona <name> loads the named persona and prepends its
37	body to the prompt as a system-prompt addendum before dispatch.
38	
39	Examples:
40	  ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
41	  ddx run --min-power 10 --max-power 20 --prompt task.md
42	  ddx run --harness codex --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
43	  ddx run --provider openrouter --model qwen3.6-27b --prompt task.md
44	  ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
45	  ddx run --profile smart --prompt task.md
46	  ddx run --text "explain this function"`,
47			RunE: f.runRun,
48		}
49	
50		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
51		cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Path to prompt file")
52		cmd.Flags().String("text", "", "Inline prompt text")
53		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Harness constraint (passthrough; not validated by ddx run)")
54		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; not validated by ddx run)")
55		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; not validated by ddx run)")
56		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
57		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
58		cmd.Flags().String("persona", "", "Persona name; body is prepended to the prompt as a system-prompt addendum")
59		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, smart")
60		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Reasoning effort override (e.g. low, medium, high)")
61		cmd.Flags().String("permissions", "", "Permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted")
62		cmd.Flags().String("timeout", "", "Request timeout (e.g. 30s, 5m)")
63		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON (alias for --output json-result)")
64		cmd.Flags().String("output", "text", "Output format: text (default), json-result, session-jsonl")
65	
66		return cmd
67	}
68	
69	func (f *CommandFactory) runRun(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
70		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
71		promptText, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("text")
72		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
73		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
74		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
75		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
76		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
77		personaName, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("persona")
78		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
79		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
80		permissions, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("permissions")
81		timeoutStr, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("timeout")
82		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
83		outputFmt, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output")
84		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
85	
86		workDir := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
87		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(workDir))
88		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(workDir, "")
89	
90		// Resolve prompt source.
91		prompt := promptText
92		promptSource := "inline"
93		if prompt == "" && promptFile == "" {
94			stat, _ := os.Stdin.Stat()
95			if (stat.Mode() & os.ModeCharDevice) == 0 {
96				data, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
97				if err != nil {
98					return fmt.Errorf("reading stdin: %w", err)
99				}
100				prompt = string(data)
101				promptSource = "stdin"
102			}
103		} else if promptFile != "" {
104			promptSource = promptFile
105		}
106	
107		// Persona injection: load body and prepend to prompt as system-prompt addendum.
108		if personaName != "" {
109			loader := persona.NewPersonaLoader(workDir)
110			p, err := loader.LoadPersona(personaName)
111			if err != nil {
112				return fmt.Errorf("persona %q: %w", personaName, err)
113			}
114			if p.Content != "" {
115				if promptFile != "" {
116					data, err := os.ReadFile(promptFile)
117					if err != nil {
118						return fmt.Errorf("reading prompt file %q: %w", promptFile, err)
119					}
120					prompt = p.Content + "\n\n" + string(data)
121					promptFile = ""
122					promptSource = personaName + "+" + promptSource
123				} else {
124					prompt = p.Content + "\n\n" + prompt
125				}
126			}
127		}
128	
129		var timeout time.Duration
130		if timeoutStr != "" {
131			var err error
132			timeout, err = time.ParseDuration(timeoutStr)
133			if err != nil {
134				return fmt.Errorf("invalid timeout: %w", err)
135			}
136		}
137	
138		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
139	
140		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
141			Harness:           harness,
142			Model:             model,
143			Provider:          provider,
144			Profile:           profile,
145			Effort:            effort,
146			Permissions:       permissions,
147			MinPower:          minPower,
148			MaxPower:          maxPower,
149			OpaquePassthrough: true,
150		}
151		if timeout > 0 {
152			overrides.Timeout = &timeout
153		}
154	
155		rcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(workDir, overrides)
156	
157		runtime := agent.AgentRunRuntime{
158			Prompt:       prompt,
159			PromptFile:   promptFile,
160			PromptSource: promptSource,
161			WorkDir:      workDir,
162		}
163	
164		result, err := agent.RunWithConfigViaService(cmd.Context(), workDir, rcfg, runtime)
165		if err != nil {
166			return err
167		}
168	
169		if asJSON {
170			outputFmt = "json-result"
171		}
172		switch outputFmt {
173		case "json-result":
174			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
175			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
176			if err := enc.Encode(result); err != nil {
177				return err
178			}
179			return runResultError(result)
180		case "session-jsonl":
181			if result.CondensedOutput != "" {
182				fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.CondensedOutput)
183			} else if result.Output != "" {
184				fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Output)
185			}
186		case "text":
187			text := agent.ExtractOutput(result.Harness, result.Output)
188			if text != "" {
189				fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), text)
190			}
191		default:
192			return fmt.Errorf("unknown --output value %q (valid: text, json-result, session-jsonl)", outputFmt)
193		}
194	
195		return runResultError(result)
196	}
197	
198	func runResultError(result *agent.Result) error {
199		if result == nil {
200			return nil
201		}
202		if result.ExitCode != 0 {
203			msg := fmt.Sprintf("agent exited with code %d", result.ExitCode)
204			if result.Error != "" {
205				msg += "\n  error: " + result.Error
206			}
207			if result.Stderr != "" && result.Stderr != result.Error {
208				lines := strings.SplitN(strings.TrimSpace(result.Stderr), "\n", 6)
209				if len(lines) > 5 {
210					lines = append(lines[:5], "  ...")
211				}
212				msg += "\n  stderr:\n    " + strings.Join(lines, "\n    ")
213			}
214			return fmt.Errorf("%s", msg)
215		}
216		return nil
217	}

34	// AttemptBackend owns the workspace and transport mechanics for one
35	// execute-bead attempt. The caller keeps prompt construction, result
36	// classification, evidence, gate evaluation, and landing in one shared path.
37	type AttemptBackend interface {
38		Name() string
39		Prepare(ctx context.Context, req AttemptBackendPrepareRequest) (*AttemptWorkspace, error)
40		Run(ctx context.Context, req AttemptBackendRunRequest) (*Result, error)
41		PublishResult(ctx context.Context, ws *AttemptWorkspace, res *ExecuteBeadResult) error
42		Cleanup(ctx context.Context, ws *AttemptWorkspace) error
43	}

77	func ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig) (AttemptBackend, error) {
78		name := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(rcfg.AttemptBackend()))
79		if name == "" {
80			name = AttemptBackendWorktree
81		}
82		switch name {
83		case AttemptBackendWorktree, "linked-worktree":
84			return WorktreeAttemptBackend{}, nil
85		case AttemptBackendLocalClone, "clone":
86			return LocalCloneAttemptBackend{CloneMode: dockerCloneMode(rcfg.ExecutionsDockerConfig())}, nil
87		case AttemptBackendDockerClone, "docker":
88			return DockerCloneAttemptBackend{Docker: rcfg.ExecutionsDockerConfig()}, nil
89		case AttemptBackendInTree:
90			return InTreeAttemptBackend{}, nil
91		default:
92			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown attempt backend %q (valid: %s, %s, %s, %s)",
93				name, AttemptBackendWorktree, AttemptBackendLocalClone, AttemptBackendDockerClone, AttemptBackendInTree)
94		}
95	}

Based on my thorough exploration of the DDx CLI codebase, here's a comprehensive summary of the current structure and execution system:

## DDx CLI Agent and Execution Architecture

### High-Level Layer Structure (FEAT-010)

The system is organized in three distinct layers:

**Layer 1: `ddx run`** - Single Fizeau Execute call
- File: `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/cmd/run.go`
- One-shot task invocation with no queue semantics
- Forwards routing knobs (harness, provider, model, min-power, max-power, profile) directly to the agent service unchanged
- No validation or pre-resolution by DDx

**Layer 2: `ddx try`** - Single bead execution through full production machinery
- File: `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/cmd/try.go` (649 lines)
- Targets one specific bead through: claim → execute → land → review
- Implements retry-power escalation policy (escalation/retry ladder)
- Has built-in worktree isolation (already implemented)
- Key features:
  - Pre-flight checks (bead lookup, claimability, unmet dependencies)
  - Post-merge review (on by default, can be skipped with break-glass flag)
  - Escalating power attempts via `runEscalatingPowerAttempts()` function
  - Exit codes: 0=success/already_satisfied, 1=preserved/no_changes, 2=failed/other

**Layer 3: `ddx work`** - Queue drain with retry-power policy
- File: `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/cmd/work.go` (363 lines)
- Drains execution-ready queue by picker priority (unlike `ddx try` which forces specific bead)
- Owns retry-power policy between attempts (min-power/max-power bounds)
- Calls `ddx try` machinery iteratively for each ready bead
- Stop conditions: drained, blocked, deferred (budget), no_progress, signal

### Worktree Isolation (Already Implemented in `ddx try`)

**Key Files:**
- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go` - Attempt backend abstraction
- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/executor.go` - Command execution with timeouts
- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/worktree_skills.go` - Worktree cleanup utilities
- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go` - Resource preflight checks

**Attempt Backends (4 options via config.ResolvedConfig.AttemptBackend()):**
1. **WorktreeAttemptBackend** (default) - Uses git worktree isolation
2. LocalCloneAttemptBackend - Full local clone with hardlinks or no-hardlinks
3. DockerCloneAttemptBackend - Docker container isolation
4. InTreeAttemptBackend - Direct in-tree execution

**WorktreeAttemptBackend Flow:**
- `Prepare()` → Creates isolated git worktree via `gitOps.WorktreeAdd()`
- Path pattern: `.execute-bead-wt-<beadID>-<attemptID>`
- `Run()` → Executes agent in isolated worktree
- `Cleanup()` → Removes worktree via `gitOps.WorktreeRemove()`

### Escalation and Retry System

**Files:**
- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go` (333 lines)
- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/executeloop/state_machine.go` - Retry state machine

**Core Loop: `runEscalatingPowerAttempts()`** (cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:288-332)
```go
func runEscalatingPowerAttempts(
    ctx context.Context,
    initialMinPower int,
    ladder escalationFloorFinder,  // power ladder
    attempt func(context.Context, int) (ExecuteBeadReport, error),
    recordAttempt func(ExecuteBeadReport),
    perBeadTracker *PerBeadCostTracker,
    allowInfrastructureRetry bool,
) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
```

**Decision Tree: `DecideAttemptTransition()`** (internal/agent/executeloop/state_machine.go:61-102)
- Input: status, detail, currentMinPower, actualPower, disrupted, budgetExhausted, allowInfrastructureRetry
- Output: Action (Stop or RetryPower) + NextMinPower floor

**Retry Triggers:**
1. **Semantic failures** → Escalate to next power ladder floor (basis = actualPower)
2. **Provider connectivity failures** → Immediate retry with minPower = actualPower + 1
3. **Budget exhaustion** → Stop
4. **Terminal status** → Stop

**Power Ladder Mechanics:**
- Managed by `escalationFloorFinder` interface (ladder.Next(actualPower))
- Stored in `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go`
- Exported via `powerladder.NewLadder(models)`

### Attempt Execution Lifecycle

**File:** `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`

**ExecuteBeadWithConfig()** flow (line 1440):
1. Resource preflight check (free disk space, inodes)
2. Generate `attemptID` (unique per attempt)
3. **Under tracker lock:**
   - Commit beads.jsonl tracker
   - Checkpoint pre-dispatch caller dirt
   - Resolve base revision
   - **Call attemptBackend.Prepare()** → Creates workspace (worktree/clone/docker)
4. Exclude cleanup metadata from git
5. Write execution cleanup metadata
6. Materialize worktree skills (remove broken symlinks)
7. Dispatch agent run via `attemptBackend.Run()`
8. Publish result via `attemptBackend.PublishResult()`
9. Cleanup workspace via `attemptBackend.Cleanup()`

### Worker Loop Implementation

**File:** `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`

**ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()** (line 1043+):
- Maintains loop state across multiple bead claims
- For each bead:
  - Claim from ready queue
  - Execute via `w.Executor.ExecuteBead()`
  - Post-attempt triage
  - Optional decomposition (epic → tasks)
  - Review & land
- Stop conditions: drained, blocked, budget, no_progress, signal
- Per-run transient candidate skips for queue consistency

### Key Configuration Points

**Config file:** Resolved via `config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)`

**AttemptBackend selection:**
```go
func ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig) (AttemptBackend, error)
```
- Default: `AttemptBackendWorktree`
- Override via `--attempt-backend` flag (try/work) or `executions.attempt_backend` config

**Execution resource checks:**
- Hard limits: 64 MB free, 1024 inodes (executionResourceMinFreeBytes/Inodes)
- Soft limits: 512 MB free, 4096 inodes (soft cleanup threshold)
- Checked before claim and worktree creation

### Summary: Try vs Work

| Aspect | `ddx try` | `ddx work` |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| **Target** | Single specific bead (forced) | Queue drain by priority |
| **Escalation** | Per-bead power ladder | Per-bead power ladder |
| **Worktree** | Yes (default) | Yes (inherited from try) |
| **Review** | Default on, break-glass skip | Default on, break-glass skip |
| **Exit codes** | 0/1/2 (try-specific) | Process exit, JSON result |
| **Stop condition** | Once bead completes | drained/blocked/budget/no_progress/signal |

The worktree isolation is **already fully implemented** for both `ddx try` and `ddx work` via the `WorktreeAttemptBackend` and configurable via the `--attempt-backend` flag or project configuration.

38	func TestDockerCloneBackendTerminatesContainerOnWorkerCancellation(t *testing.T) {
39		// Verify context cancellation or graceful worker shutdown stops the docker CLI process
40		// and the associated containerized harness tree.
41		// Note: This test verifies the mechanism without requiring an actual docker daemon.
42		// The actual container cleanup is guaranteed by the --rm flag in dockerRunArgs.
43	
44		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
45	
46		// Create a docker command that will be cancelled
47		cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(ctx, "ps")
48	
49		// Cancel the context to simulate worker shutdown
50		time.AfterFunc(10*time.Millisecond, cancel)
51	
52		// The command should respect context cancellation through exec.CommandContext
53		err := cmd.Run()
54	
55		// We expect an error since we cancelled the context
56		require.Error(t, err, "docker command should be interrupted by context cancellation")
57	
58		// Verify the context was actually cancelled
59		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
60			return ctx.Err() != nil
61		}, 100*time.Millisecond, 5*time.Millisecond, "context should be cancelled")
62	}

64	func TestDockerCloneBackendParentDeathCleanupDocumentedOrEnforced(t *testing.T) {
65		// Verify abnormal parent-death behavior for docker-clone is either enforced
66		// through parent-death/process-group/container cleanup or has a documented justification.
67	
68		cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "ps")
69	
70		// Verify that process-group setup is in place for docker command
71		require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker command must have SysProcAttr")
72		require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "Setpgid must be set for process-group isolation")
73		require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "Pdeathsig must be set to SIGKILL for parent-death cleanup")
74	
75		// Verify the docker run arguments include --rm for automatic container cleanup

85		require.Contains(t, args, "--rm", "docker run must include --rm for automatic container cleanup on exit")
86		require.Contains(t, args, "--init", "docker run must include --init for proper signal handling inside container")

```go
type WorktreeAttemptBackend struct{}

func (WorktreeAttemptBackend) Name() string { return AttemptBackendWorktree }

func (WorktreeAttemptBackend) Prepare(ctx context.Context, req AttemptBackendPrepareRequest) (*AttemptWorkspace, error)
  - Creates worktree directory parent
  - Calls gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev)
  - Returns AttemptWorkspace with gitOps reference

func (WorktreeAttemptBackend) Run(ctx context.Context, req AttemptBackendRunRequest) (*Result, error)

func (WorktreeAttemptBackend) PublishResult(...)
  - No-op (returns nil)

func (WorktreeAttemptBackend) Cleanup(ctx context.Context, ws *AttemptWorkspace) error
  - Calls gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, workDir)
```

```go
type worktreeAddFailGitOps struct {
    baseRev     string
    addedPath   string
    removedPath string
}

// Each interface method returns canned values or errors
func (g *worktreeAddFailGitOps) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {
    g.addedPath = wtPath
    if err := os.MkdirAll(wtPath, 0o755); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(wtPath, "partial.txt"), ...) {
        return err
    }
    return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add: No space left on device")
```

### Error Handling Pattern

The cleanup is robust:
- Cleanup errors are ignored in deferred cleanup (line 1568: `_ = attemptBackend.Cleanup(...)`)
- WorktreeRemove always returns nil, even on errors (idempotent cleanup)
- Partial directory cleanup happens on Prepare failures (worktreeAddFailGitOps test case)

This design ensures failed attempt backends don't block the execute loop and cleanup is always attempted even if partially successful.

25	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
26	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
27	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
28		parent := os.Environ()
29		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
30		for _, kv := range parent {
31			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
32				continue
33			}
34			env = append(env, kv)
35		}
36		return env
37	}

39	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
40	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
41	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
42		t.Helper()
43		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
44		cmd.Dir = dir
45		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
46		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
47		if err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
49		}
50		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
51	}

144	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
145	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
146	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
147	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
148	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
149		landSerializerMu.Lock()
150		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
151		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
152		if !ok {
153			m = &sync.Mutex{}
154			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
155		}
156		return m

159	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
160	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
161	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
162	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
163	//
164	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
165	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
166	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
167	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
168	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
169	//
170	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
171	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
172	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
173		t.Helper()
174		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
175		// calls share a stable test runner instance.
176		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
177		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
178		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
179		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
180		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
181		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
182	
183		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{
184			Model: directivePath,
185		})
186		rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{Harness: "script"})
187	
188		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
189			repoMu.Lock()
190			defer repoMu.Unlock()
191	
192			res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
193				AgentRunner: runner,
194			}, gitOps)
195			if err != nil {
196				if res != nil {
197					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
198				}
199				return ExecuteBeadReport{
200					BeadID: beadID,
201					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
202					Detail: err.Error(),
203				}, nil
204			}
205	
206			landFromRev := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
207			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
208				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
209					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
210					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
211					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
212				},

434	func formatConcurrentLandingDiagnostics(projectRoot, ddxDir string, results []*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, errs []error) string {
435		var b strings.Builder
436	
437		b.WriteString("workers:\n")
438		for i := 0; i < maxLandingDiagInt(len(results), len(errs)); i++ {
439			var (
440				result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
441				err    error
442			)
443			if i < len(results) {
444				result = results[i]
445			}
446			if i < len(errs) {
447				err = errs[i]
448			}
449			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  worker-%d err=%v", i, err))
450			if result == nil {
451				b.WriteString(" result=<nil>\n")
452				continue
453			}
454			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
455				" attempts=%d successes=%d failures=%d stop=%q exit=%q no_ready=%t no_ready_detail=%+v\n",
456				result.Attempts,
457				result.Successes,
458				result.Failures,
459				result.StopCondition,
460				result.ExitReason,
461				result.NoReadyWork,
462				result.NoReadyWorkDetail,
463			))
464			for _, report := range result.Results {
465				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
466					"    bead=%s status=%s detail=%q attempt=%s result_rev=%s landed_rev=%s preserve_ref=%s\n",
467					report.BeadID,
468					report.Status,
469					report.Detail,
470					report.AttemptID,
471					report.ResultRev,
472					report.LandedRev,
473					report.PreserveRef,
474				))
475			}
476		}
477	
478		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
479		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
480		if err != nil {
481			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("beads: list error: %v\n", err))
482		} else {
483			sort.Slice(all, func(i, j int) bool { return all[i].ID < all[j].ID })
484			closedCount := 0
485			b.WriteString("bead lifecycle summary:\n")
486			for _, item := range all {
487				if item.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
488					closedCount++
489				}
490				events, eventsErr := store.Events(item.ID)
491				lastKinds := ""
492				if eventsErr != nil {
493					lastKinds = "events-error:" + eventsErr.Error()
494				} else {
495					lastKinds = tailEventKinds(events, 3)
496				}
497				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
498					"  %s status=%s owner=%s closing_commit_sha=%s work_last_status=%s events=%d last_events=%s\n",
499					item.ID,
500					item.Status,
501					item.Owner,
502					extraString(item.Extra, "closing_commit_sha"),
503					extraString(item.Extra, "work-last-status"),
504					len(events),
505					lastKinds,
506				))
507			}
508			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  closed_count=%d total=%d\n", closedCount, len(all)))
509			if breakdown, breakdownErr := store.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); breakdownErr != nil {
510				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("queue breakdown error: %v\n", breakdownErr))
511			} else {
512				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
513					"queue breakdown: ready=%v dependency_waiting=%v proposed=%v retry_cooldown=%v external_blocked=%v not_eligible=%v superseded=%v epics=%v epic_closure_candidates=%v next_retry_after=%s human_review_blocked_total=%d\n",
514					breakdown.ExecutionReady,
515					breakdown.DependencyWaiting,
516					breakdown.ProposedOperatorAttention,
517					breakdown.RetryCooldown,
518					breakdown.ExternalBlocked,
519					breakdown.NotEligible,
520					breakdown.Superseded,
521					breakdown.Epics,
522					breakdown.EpicClosureCandidates,
523					breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
524					breakdown.HumanReviewBlockedTotal,
525				))
526			}
527		}
528	
529		b.WriteString("git refs:\n")
530		head, headErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
531		b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  HEAD=%s err=%v\n", strings.TrimSpace(head), headErr))
532		refs, refsErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname) %(objectname)", "refs/heads", "refs/ddx/iterations")
533		if refsErr != nil {
534			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  for-each-ref error: %v\n", refsErr))
535		} else if strings.TrimSpace(refs) == "" {
536			b.WriteString("  (no refs)\n")
537		} else {
538			for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(refs), "\n") {
539				b.WriteString("  " + line + "\n")
540			}
541		}
542	
543		return b.String()
544	}
545	
546	func extraString(extra map[string]any, key string) string {
547		if extra == nil {
548			return ""
549		}
550		raw, ok := extra[key]
551		if !ok || raw == nil {
552			return ""
553		}
554		return strings.TrimSpace(fmt.Sprint(raw))
555	}
556	
557	func tailEventKinds(events []bead.BeadEvent, n int) string {
558		if len(events) == 0 {
559			return "(none)"
560		}
561		if n > len(events) {
562			n = len(events)
563		}
564		kinds := make([]string, 0, n)
565		for _, event := range events[len(events)-n:] {
566			kinds = append(kinds, event.Kind)
567		}
568		return strings.Join(kinds, ",")
569	}
570	
571	func maxLandingDiagInt(a, b int) int {
572		if a > b {
573			return a
574		}
575		return b
576	}
